01 · Engineering snapshot

Where I spend
my time.

Technical density over decoration. Real bench work, real constraints, real debugging.

Current focus

Vehicle electronics + driver interface

MCU-based dashboard, steering wheel inputs, CAN telemetry, SD logging, and bench validation with PSU and CAN simulator.

Battery work

BMS algorithm analysis

SoC, SoH, SoP review, drive-cycle data, CAN log interpretation, and LFP literature research under real vehicle conditions.

Digital systems

FPGA + embedded teaching

Digital logic support, Artix-7 labs, Vivado simulations, and student debugging workflows for a full semester.

02 · Featured case study

Steering wheel
input & display.

Bench demo — CAN live data
PCB layout — steering wheel
FSAE EV — Binghamton Motorsports
Capstone · Formula SAE EV

Modular steering wheel input and dashboard display system

Primary portfolio project

Replaces a slower dashboard approach by moving driver-facing telemetry and control inputs onto a dedicated MCU-based steering wheel module. Boot fast, show useful data, survive vibration.

Embedded C/C++LVGLSquareLine Studio CANSPI TFTSD loggingFSAE EV
What I owned
  • Button matrix and rotary encoder input behavior
  • LVGL / SquareLine display integration
  • CAN message send/receive with simulator testing
  • SD logging marker concept and display-side storage
  • Bench validation using PSU, Teensy simulator, serial diagnostics
Evidence to show
  • Architecture diagram: MCU, TFT, CAN transceiver, SD
  • CAN ID table: buttons, encoders, telemetry
  • Boot-time measurement and current draw
  • GIF of screen switching via two of six buttons
  • Wiring harness / PCB / wheel assembly photo
03 · Selected projects

Other work
worth showing.

P.01

Autonomous Minesweeper Robot

Pololu 3pi+ robot using embedded control logic to navigate a 5×5 grid, simulate mines, and choose safe movement decisions via FSM.

Embedded CFSMK-mapsRobotics
P.02

RF Drone Detection GUI

Python interface for FFT-based RF signal analysis and operator-facing threat classification during anti-drone internship work.

PythonFFTRFGUI
P.03

BMS Algorithm Review

Public-safe writeup around SoC, SoH, and SoP estimation strategies for LFP batteries using literature and anonymized workflow diagrams.

BMSSoC / SoHPythonLFP
P.04

Digital Logic + FPGA Work

Vivado simulations, Artix-7 FPGA debugging, Verilog/VHDL labs, and course assistant experience supporting student projects.

VivadoFPGAVerilogArtix-7
04 · Experience

Where I've
worked.

Jun 2025 — Present
Battery Management Systems Intern
Tata Motors EV · Pune, India
  • Analyzed SoC, SoP, and SoH strategies using battery and drive-cycle data.
  • Debugged CAN data streams from pack-level and cell-level sensors.
  • Researched BMS estimation limitations under aggressive discharge and real vehicle behavior.
Jan 2024 — Present
Low Voltage Electronics Engineer · Race Engineer · Driver
Binghamton FSAE Electric
  • Worked on low-voltage architecture, driver-facing electronics, ECU support, BMS tuning, and motor controller integration.
  • Led steering wheel/dashboard electronics development focused on boot time, telemetry, and usable driver controls.
Aug 2024 — Dec 2024
Course Assistant — Digital Logic Design
Binghamton University
  • Supported FPGA-based labs with Xilinx Artix-7 boards, Vivado simulations, and Verilog debugging.
Jun 2023 — Jul 2023
Software Engineering Project Intern
Paras Anti-Drone Technologies · Mumbai
  • Built FFT-based RF analysis tools and a real-time operator interface for drone-threat identification.
05 · Tools & systems

Things I
know well.

Embedded

Embedded C/C++, Arduino, LVGL, SquareLine Studio, UART, SPI, I2C, CAN, SD logging.

Hardware

PCB layout, Fusion 360, soldering, oscilloscopes, bench PSUs, CAN transceivers.

Vehicle EE

Low-voltage EV systems, BMS support, motor controller integration, FSAE safety workflows.

Analysis

Python, MATLAB/Simulink, Simscape, drive-cycle analysis, battery literature review.

Digital logic

Vivado, Artix-7 FPGA boards, Verilog/VHDL, waveform debugging, lab instruction.

CAD

Steering wheel packaging, display mounting, quick release constraints, test-oriented enclosures.

Motorsport

Race engineering, driver feedback, telemetry priorities, cockpit usability, track-side debugging.

Improving

Cleaner public documentation, project writeups, better diagrams, and code release quality.
